key_insight: MTP rollout speed in RL is limited by entropy growth, but rejection sampling plus an end-to-end TV loss can preserve high acceptance and restore training speed.
engineering_relevance: The method provides a practical way to accelerate large-scale RL post-training pipelines for reasoning, code, and agentic LLMs without frequent online MTP updates.
method: Model the drop in MTP acceptance as a consequence of entropy fluctuation during RL.
Replace greedy draft sampling with probabilistic rejection sampling to reduce entropy disturbance.
Train with an end-to-end TV loss that directly optimizes multi-step rejection sampling acceptance rate.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic tasks, the approach improved acceptance rates by about 10%, reached up to 95% acceptance, and delivered up to 1.8x end-to-end async RL acceleration on Qwen3.5/3.6/3.7 models.
watch_item: The gains depend on integrating rejection sampling and TV-based training correctly, and performance may vary with model entropy dynamics and RL setup.
paper_title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
